calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-06-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurence Blau for and with Geoffrey Gordon-Creed.

The Court stated that the fees paid to the CPA were part of the duties of the administrator and were usually deducted from the administrators commissions. Mr. Blau reported that the decedent owned 90% of a scaffolding business, that they had to run the business and handle the sale, and that Mr. Murphy was used for the evaluation of the business and the negotiations of the sale. In addition, he reported that they had received 3 out of the 7 receipts, but that all of the checks had been cashed. Mr. Gordon-Creed explained that the bulk of the work was in regards to the negotiations of the business which was lawyer work not administrator work, and that it was more cost effective for him to have performed the service rather than hire another attorney. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-08-2008 Continued To Feb-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition By Robert S. Castle, An Heir And Interested Person, For Order Modifying Directions To The Administrator By Order Entered March 16, 2007; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Jul-02-2007 Granted Per Written Order

Verified Petn. By Robert S. Castel, And Heri And Interested Person, For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Jun-11-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for and with petitioner Robert Castle. Laurence Blau for Geoffrey Gordon-Creed, administrator. Mr. Albright is not in court. He has signed the stipulation. This Court can not sign the order with the same issue that is on Appeal and to modify a Family Court order. Mr. Canatella will dismiss the declaratory relief petition and he will dismiss the Appeal. Court wants a new petition to modify the order after the appeal is dismissed. The Declaratory Relief petition is dismissed per Mr. Canatella's request.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Arias.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Consideration Of A Preliminary Distribution To Robert Castle And The Effect Of A Levy On His Interest
Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella (Counsel for Robert Castle, Beneficiary); Dennis Albright (Counsel for Judy Castle, Lienor); Laurence Blau (Counsel for Geoffrey Gordon-Creed, Personal Representative).

Mr. Canatella and Mr. Albright presented oral argument as to the nature of the claim (the Court categorically denying that Ms. Castle's levy was in the nature of a creditor's claim since it was not a claim against the decedent) and as to whether the levy was procedurally in order. The Court took the matter under submission.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurence Blau (Counsel for Geoffrey Gordon-Creed, Personal Representative); Richard Canatella (Counsel for Robert Castle, beneficiary).

After brief discussion concerning the levy against Mr. Castle's interest in the estate and the effect of community property on the calculation of fees , the Court granted the petition approving the accounting and authorizing the preliminary distribution except as to Robert Castle (signing the Order for same in open court). The preliminary distribution as to Mr. Castle and the levy thereon will be addressed at hearing on March 12, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
